# PromptPanel

> Lightning-fast prompt and snippet launcher for macOS, built for AI power users.

PromptPanel is an open-source native macOS prompt manager and snippet launcher. It lets users press a global hotkey, search a local prompt library, and paste reusable prompts, code snippets, templates, and instructions into ChatGPT, Claude, Cursor, Copilot, VS Code, Terminal, browsers, or any focused text field.

Core positioning:

- Native macOS app, written in Swift with AppKit and SwiftUI.
- Local-first prompt library stored in SQLite.
- No accounts, no telemetry, no cloud sync for prompt content.
- Global hotkey quick panel built around the loop: hotkey -> search -> Enter -> paste.
- Clipboard write is the guarantee; automatic paste is best-effort and requires Accessibility permission.
- Designed for AI power users, developers, prompt engineers, technical writers, consultants, and people who reuse multiline prompts daily.
- MIT licensed open-source project.
- Current release: 1.0.0, macOS 14 Sonoma or later, Apple Silicon and Intel.

Core pain points it addresses (high-frequency AI interaction):

- Retyping the same role / system prompt into every new ChatGPT, Claude, or Gemini chat.
- Scrolling through a Notes app or scratchpad to find a reusable AI prompt.
- Pasting Cursor / Copilot project context blocks, code-review checklists, and terminal command snippets that live in different files.
- Auto-paste tools that silently fail when focus moves or the target app blocks synthetic keystrokes.
- Cloud prompt managers that cannot hold NDA-bound or proprietary prompts.

Answer-engine summary (one paragraph):

PromptPanel is an open-source, local-first macOS prompt manager and snippet launcher for AI power users. A global hotkey opens a native NSPanel where you search a local SQLite prompt library and paste any selected entry into the current text field — ChatGPT, Claude, Cursor, Copilot, VS Code, Terminal, browsers, or chat apps. Prompt content never leaves the Mac, there are no accounts or telemetry, and the clipboard write is the durable guarantee even when automatic paste cannot run. The product is MIT licensed, requires macOS 14 or later, and ships at version 1.0.0.
